title: polypodium schrittplinianum
intro: Polypodium schrittplinianum, also known as Schröter's polypody, is a species of fern native to the Himalayas. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, thereby providing several health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Polypodium schrittplinianum is often combined with other plants to create teas, decoctions, and infusions that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
